name: KeyAnalyzer
javadoc:
/** * Defines the interface to analyze {@link org.apache.commons.collections4.Trie Trie} keys on a bit level. * {@link KeyAnalyzer}'s methods return the length of the key in bits, whether or not a bit is set, * and bits per element in the key. *

* Additionally, a method determines if a key is a prefix of another * key and returns the bit index where one key is different from another * key (if the key and found key are equal than the return value is * {@link #EQUAL_BIT_KEY}). * * @since 4.0 * @version $Id: KeyAnalyzer.java 1491621 2013-06-10 22:05:38Z tn $ */

isEqualBitKey
isNullBitKey
isValidBitIndex
bitsPerElement
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns the number of bits per element in the key.
 * This is only useful for variable-length keys, such as Strings.
 *
 * @return the number of bits per element
 */

tags.assertion: assert methodResultID == 32; // Assuming the default value is 32
tags.description: the number of bits per element in the key.

members.code:
/**
 * Returns the number of bits per element in the key.
 * This is only useful for variable-length keys, such as Strings.
 *
 * @return the number of bits per element
 */
public abstract int bitsPerElement();

members.code:
/**
 * Returns the length of the Key in bits.
 *
 * @param key  the key
 * @return the bit length of the key
 */
public abstract int lengthInBits(K key);

members.name: isBitSet
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns whether or not a bit is set.
 *
 * @param key  the key to check, may not be null
 * @param bitIndex  the bit index to check
 * @param lengthInBits  the maximum key length in bits to check
 * @return {@code true} if the bit is set in the given key and
 *   {@code bitIndex} < {@code lengthInBits}, {@code false} otherwise.
 */

members.code:
/**
 * Returns whether or not a bit is set.
 *
 * @param key  the key to check, may not be null
 * @param bitIndex  the bit index to check
 * @param lengthInBits  the maximum key length in bits to check
 * @return {@code true} if the bit is set in the given key and
 *   {@code bitIndex} < {@code lengthInBits}, {@code false} otherwise.
 */
public abstract boolean isBitSet(K key, int bitIndex, int lengthInBits);

members.name: bitIndex
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns the n-th different bit between key and other. This starts the comparison in
 * key at 'offsetInBits' and goes for 'lengthInBits' bits, and compares to the other key starting
 * at 'otherOffsetInBits' and going for 'otherLengthInBits' bits.
 *
 * @param key  the key to use
 * @param offsetInBits  the bit offset in the key
 * @param lengthInBits  the maximum key length in bits to use
 * @param other  the other key to use
 * @param otherOffsetInBits  the bit offset in the other key
 * @param otherLengthInBits  the maximum key length in bits for the other key
 * @return the bit index where the key and other first differ
 */

tags.name: offsetInBits
tags.content: the bit offset in the key
tags.description: the bit offset in the key

tags.name: lengthInBits
tags.content: the maximum key length in bits to use
tags.description: the maximum key length in bits to use

tags.name: otherOffsetInBits
tags.content: the bit offset in the other key
tags.description: the bit offset in the other key

tags.name: otherLengthInBits
tags.content: the maximum key length in bits for the other key
tags.description: the maximum key length in bits for the other key

tags.content: bit index where the key and other first differ
tags.assertion: assert receiverObjectID.bitIndex(args[0], args[1], args[2], args[3], args[4], args[5]) == result;
tags.description: the bit index where the key and other first differ

members.code:
/**
 * Returns the n-th different bit between key and other. This starts the comparison in
 * key at 'offsetInBits' and goes for 'lengthInBits' bits, and compares to the other key starting
 * at 'otherOffsetInBits' and going for 'otherLengthInBits' bits.
 *
 * @param key  the key to use
 * @param offsetInBits  the bit offset in the key
 * @param lengthInBits  the maximum key length in bits to use
 * @param other  the other key to use
 * @param otherOffsetInBits  the bit offset in the other key
 * @param otherLengthInBits  the maximum key length in bits for the other key
 * @return the bit index where the key and other first differ
 */
public abstract int bitIndex(K key, int offsetInBits, int lengthInBits, K other, int otherOffsetInBits, int otherLengthInBits);

members.name: isPrefix
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Determines whether or not the given prefix (from offset to length) is a prefix of the given key.
 *
 * @param prefix  the prefix to check
 * @param offsetInBits  the bit offset in the key
 * @param lengthInBits  the maximum key length in bits to use
 * @param key  the key to check
 * @return {@code true} if this is a valid prefix for the given key
 */

tags.name: offsetInBits
tags.content: the bit offset in the key
tags.description: the bit offset in the key

tags.name: lengthInBits
tags.content: the maximum key length in bits to use
tags.description: the maximum key length in bits to use

tags.content: true} if this is a valid prefix for the given key
tags.description: true if this is a valid prefix for the given key

members.code:
/**
 * Determines whether or not the given prefix (from offset to length) is a prefix of the given key.
 *
 * @param prefix  the prefix to check
 * @param offsetInBits  the bit offset in the key
 * @param lengthInBits  the maximum key length in bits to use
 * @param key  the key to check
 * @return {@code true} if this is a valid prefix for the given key
 */
public abstract boolean isPrefix(K prefix, int offsetInBits, int lengthInBits, K key);
